A1..A5.A1_authoritative_source: authoritative source (official HF livecodebench/* dataset OR the official LiveCodeBench GitHub repo; NOT an aggregator / mirror / leaderboard-site intro / blog rumor / 'planned' announcement)
A1..A5.A2_dated_problems: dated problems (each carries a contest_date time-anchor)
A1..A5.A3_functional_compatible: functional/code-generation-compatible (has a starter_code FUNCTIONAL subset the W89 mechanism can attack)
A1..A5.A4_sha_pinnable_admittable: machine-checkable provenance (a SHA-256-pinnable JSONL artifact that can be operator-fetched + pinned + admitted to LIVECODEBENCH_KNOWN_RELEASES)
A1..A5.A5_reproducible_histogram: reproducible date histogram (the functional month histogram can be re-derived from the pinned bytes)

construction_rule.B1_authoritative_construction_provenance: authoritative construction provenance: the candidate's problem set is fully defined by an LCB-PUBLISHED artifact (a livecodebench/* HF dataset revision/commit/PR OR the official LCB GitHub data-generation pipeline + a published problem-id/URL manifest); a raw-contest-site scrape / operator hand-selection / non-LCB-published assembly is NOT
construction_rule.B2_no_operator_curation: no operator curation (reproducibility): the selection AND ordering of problems is fully determined by the published provenance, so anyone re-running it obtains the byte-identical set; the operator contributes NO discretionary choice (the anti-cherry-pick / no-vibes criterion)

provenance_surfaces.finding: 20 commits; latest data-bearing = 'add v6' 2025-04-21; HEAD = 'fix typos (#4)' 2025-06-05.
provenance_surfaces.note: No post-v6 data commit/revision; no test7. The revision history itself (not just the file tree) confirms v6 is the newest data.
No staging / preview / v7 branch or tag carrying newer data.

provenance_surfaces.finding: Newest threads = 'LCB pull request' #14 + 'Clarification on v6 size (454 vs 175)' #13; older = parquet/loader-script issues.
provenance_surfaces.note: No v7 / newer-data / post-Apr-2025 discussion or PR proposing new problems.

provenance_surfaces.finding: Newest 2025-07-16 ('fix: typo in Explorer URL'); recent activity = runner maintenance (model infos, gemini, ERRATA, util fixes).
provenance_surfaces.note: No data-collection commit; no v7. The repo is the runner/eval harness, not a data-collection pipeline.

provenance_surfaces.finding: Top-level = lcb_runner/ + assets/ + configs (pyproject, uv.lock, ...); NO scraper / data / collect / contest directory.
provenance_surfaces.note: The public repo publishes NO data-generation/collection pipeline => no published mechanism from which a post-v6 slice could be reproducibly CONSTRUCTED.

provenance_surfaces.finding: Provenance = LeetCode / AtCoder / Codeforces contests; each release_vN a temporal snapshot (README prose lags at v5). Documents ONLY loading published releases.
provenance_surfaces.note: No generation tool / script / problem-id manifest documented for collecting NEW problems => the published releases ARE the provenance.

provenance_surfaces.finding: Loads EXCLUSIVELY via load_dataset('livecodebench/code_generation_lite', version_tag=...); no local scraping.
provenance_surfaces.note: The provenance flows contest-sites -> LCB's (private, unpublished) collection -> HF release -> runner. Construction can only enter at the HF-release level, which requires LCB to publish it.

proposal_admissibility.label: raw-contest hand-assembly (post-2025-04)
proposal_admissibility.reason: CONSTRUCTION_INADMISSIBLE [A1_authoritative fail]: The ONLY post-v6 path that exists today. A SHA-pinnable, dated, functional JSONL CAN be assembled from raw contests — but it is REFUSED: not an LCB-published provenance artifact (B1) and operator-curated / not reproducible from a published manifest (B2); also not the official livecodebench/* source (A1). Selling it as a LiveCodeBench-grade resistant instrument would be exactly the vibes-based cherry-picking the discipline forbids.

proposal_admissibility.label: LCB-published collection pipeline + manifest (template)
proposal_admissibility.reason: CONSTRUCTION_ADMISSIBLE_BUT_ARTIFACT_ABSENT: LCB-published collection pipeline + manifest (template) WOULD be construction-admissible (A1..A5 ∧ B1 ∧ B2, post-frontier) — but its LCB-published provenance artifact does not exist on the live surface ((hypothetical) official LCB GitHub data-generation pipeline + a published post-2025-04 problem-id/URL manifest). This names the exact missing upstream artifact (RUNBOOK § 3 + § 7); not buildable now.

construction_attempt.missing_artifact: An LCB-published post-v6 provenance artifact: a livecodebench/code_generation(_lite) dataset revision/commit/PR adding functional problems dated strictly after 2025-04-05, OR the official LCB GitHub data-generation pipeline + a published problem-id/URL manifest enabling reproducible (B1 ∧ B2) construction. Verified ABSENT at all eight provenance surfaces (commit log / refs / discussions / GitHub commits / GitHub tags / repo pipeline structure / README / runner loader). The only post-v6 path (raw-contest hand-assembly) is refused by A1 ∧ B1 ∧ B2.

admissibility_rule.A1_authoritative_source: authoritative source (official HF livecodebench/* dataset OR the official LiveCodeBench GitHub repo; NOT an aggregator / mirror / leaderboard-site intro / blog rumor / 'planned' announcement)
admissibility_rule.A2_dated_problems: dated problems (each carries a contest_date time-anchor)
admissibility_rule.A3_functional_compatible: functional/code-generation-compatible (has a starter_code FUNCTIONAL subset the W89 mechanism can attack)
admissibility_rule.A4_sha_pinnable_admittable: machine-checkable provenance (a SHA-256-pinnable JSONL artifact that can be operator-fetched + pinned + admitted to LIVECODEBENCH_KNOWN_RELEASES)
admissibility_rule.A5_reproducible_histogram: reproducible date histogram (the functional month histogram can be re-derived from the pinned bytes)

instrument_admissibility.label: release_v6 (admitted instrument)
instrument_admissibility.reason: ADMISSIBLE_BUT_NOT_NEWER: release_v6 (admitted instrument) is A1..A5-admissible but does not post-date the admitted instrument (version<= 6 and frontier<= 2025-04-05). The current admitted instrument: admissible but NOT new (functional frontier 2025-04-05, already the certification base).

instrument_admissibility.label: full code_generation dataset
instrument_admissibility.reason: ADMISSIBLE_BUT_NOT_NEWER: full code_generation dataset is A1..A5-admissible but does not post-date the admitted instrument (version<= 6 and frontier<= 2025-04-05). The full (non-lite) dataset: authoritative + dated + functional, but its README frontier is the SAME Apr-2025 => admissible-as-source but NOT newer than the admitted lite v6.

instrument_admissibility.label: release_latest alias
instrument_admissibility.reason: ADMISSIBLE_BUT_NOT_NEWER: release_latest alias is A1..A5-admissible but does not post-date the admitted instrument (version<= 6 and frontier<= 2025-04-05). The upstream loader's 'latest' alias resolves to the SAME six files as release_v6 => no hidden newer-than-v6 supply.

instrument_admissibility.label: planned release_v7 (search rumor)
instrument_admissibility.reason: NOT_ADMISSIBLE [A1_authoritative,A2_dated,A3_functional,A4_sha_pinnable,A5_histogram fail]: A 'planned v7' mentioned only by a non-primary summary; contradicted by all four authoritative surfaces; no artifact, no SHA => REFUSED by A1 + A4. Recorded as the W117 watch signal.

notes: no upstream change vs baseline (frontier unchanged)

DeepSeek official sources (no V4 cutoff disclosed; V3 system-prompt extraction ~Jul-2024 is non-official) => UNKNOWN. No official V4 training cutoff published. V4 post-dates V3 (2024-12), so an estimable cutoff is >= early-2025 => UNKNOWN.

Released 2026-03-16, which post-dates the ENTIRE release_v6 window => exposed even if a cutoff were disclosed => UNKNOWN.

decision.w115_blocker: The latest resistant FUNCTIONAL instrument (release_v6; functional 2025-01-11..2025-04-05) does not post-date a single reachable stronger-than-Maverick model's VERIFIABLE cutoff. A >= 30 functional resistant slice requires a KNOWN cutoff <= ~2025-01; the reachable frontier models (Qwen3-Coder-480B 2025-07, DeepSeek-V4-pro 2025+, Mistral-Small-4 2026-03) have OFFICIALLY UNDISCLOSED cutoffs (C1) and, where estimable, cutoffs at/after the Apr-2025 frontier (C2) — the gaps COMPOUND. Maverick is the only KNOWN cutoff and is already SETTLED here (W113). => NO new stronger-model resistant pilot is certifiable on the latest real data.
decision.next_instrument_requirement: A resistant FUNCTIONAL instrument with >= 30 problems dated strictly after a reachable frontier model's KNOWN cutoff. Concretely: (a) a future LCB release_v7+ (or equivalent freshly-dated functional benchmark) with post-2025-04 functional problems, operator-fetched + SHA-pinned AND admitted to the loader; AND (b) a reachable stronger-than-Maverick model whose cutoff is officially DISCLOSED (KNOWN) and earlier than those problems. Neither holds as of 2026-05-29.

w116_fire_condition.cutoff_trigger: A reachable stronger-than-Maverick model discloses a KNOWN cutoff month <= 2025-01 (so the current release_v6 admits a >= 30 resistant slice for it); update MODEL_TRAINING_CUTOFFS + W114_CUTOFF_PROVENANCE, then re-run.

w117_fire_condition.cutoff_trigger: A reachable stronger-than-Maverick model discloses, from a PRIMARY source, a KNOWN cutoff month <= 2025-01 (so the current release_v6 admits a >= 30 resistant slice for it); update MODEL_TRAINING_CUTOFFS + W114_CUTOFF_PROVENANCE, then re-run.
